4.2Configuration
4.2.1Frontmatter4.2.2Themes4.2.3Layouts
4.3Directives

Frontmatter

Frontmatter controls page-level metadata and overrides.

Frontmatter applies to a single .mq file.
Site-wide defaults are defined in marque.toml.

Basic shape

yaml
---
title: Frontmatter
nav: frontmatter
layout: sidebar
theme: default
width: wide
---

Supported keys

title

  • Used for page display label fallback.
  • Used in the document title in <head>.
yaml
title: Quick Start

nav

  • Controls output filename/route slug.
  • nav: quickstart -> quickstart.html.
yaml
nav: quickstart

layout

  • Per-page layout override.
  • Typical values: topnav, sidebar.
yaml
layout: sidebar

theme

  • Per-page theme override.
  • Can be any built-in or custom theme name.
yaml
theme: pycorino

width

  • Page content width override.
  • Accepts: narrow, normal, wide, full, or percent values.
yaml
width: wide
# or
width: 86
# or
width: 86%

Precedence

  1. Frontmatter values override marque.toml for that page.
  2. Missing keys fall back to marque.toml.

Common mistakes

  • Missing opening/closing --- fence.
  • Reusing the same nav slug across multiple pages.
  • Renaming nav without updating links and summary entries.

summary.mq controls navigation order and labels.
Frontmatter does not define nav ordering.
